form.label: Settings
form.description: Bootstrap defaults applied to NEW entities at discovery time, app health checks, and the audit log of inline edits. Existing entities keep their per-row thresholds until you edit them.

panel.title: Current settings (legacy KV, not read by discovery)

search.query: | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | sort + setting_key | table _key, setting_key, setting_value

div.p: Discovery + status defaults are now stored as Splunk search macros, not KV-store settings. Splunk 10.4's parser rejects the subsearch-in-eval pattern this dashboard used to rely on. Edit the values directly via:
Settings › Advanced search › Search macros — filter for carbide_default_.

ul.li: carbide_default_monitored — 0 (off) or 1 (on) for newly discovered entities

div.p: The carbide_settings KV collection still exists and its contents show below for reference, but discovery no longer reads it. The rows are harmless — leftover from the old setting-lookup mechanism.

search.query: | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_settings collection reachable" | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_hosts_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_tracked_hosts collection reachable" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_sources_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_tracked_sources collection reachable" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_entity_filters collection reachable (seeded if > 0)" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_seed | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_entity_filters_seed CSV present" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_holidays_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_holidays collection reachable" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_assets_lookup | stats count as rows | eval check="carbide_assets collection reachable" ] | append [ | tstats count as rows where index=`carbide_index` earliest=-7d@d | eval check="dedicated index `carbide_index` reachable + recent snapshots" ] | append [ | tstats count as rows where `carbide_entity_filter("hosts")` earliest=-1h@h | eval check="carbide_entity_filter(\"hosts\") macro returns a valid clause" ] | eval status = case( check="carbide_entity_filters collection reachable (seeded if > 0)" AND rows>0, "ok", check="carbide_entity_filters collection reachable (seeded if > 0)", "warn (empty)", check="carbide_entity_filters_seed CSV present" AND rows>0, "ok", check="carbide_entity_filters_seed CSV present", "fail", check="dedicated index `carbide_index` reachable + recent snapshots" AND rows>0, "ok", check="dedicated index `carbide_index` reachable + recent snapshots", "warn (no events in 7d)", isnotnull(rows), "ok", 1=1, "fail" ) | table check, status, rows

| rest /servicesNS/-/carbide_app_for_splunk/saved/searches splunk_server=local | search title="Carbide -*" | eval next_run = if(isnull(next_scheduled_time) OR next_scheduled_time="", "n/a", next_scheduled_time) | table title cron_schedule disabled is_scheduled next_run | rename title as "saved search"

| makeresults | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_hosts_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_tracked_hosts" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_tracked_sources_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_tracked_sources" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_settings_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_settings" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_entity_filters_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_entity_filters" ] | append [ | inputlookup carbide_status_history_lookup | stats count as rows | eval collection="carbide_status_history" ] | where isnotnull(collection) | table collection rows

panel.title: Audit trail (every KV write under this app)

html.p: Sourced from Splunk's built-in splunkd access log (index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access) — every REST call to the Carbide collections is recorded automatically with the caller and the URI. No app-side audit code required.

search.query: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access uri_path="*/storage/collections/data/carbide_*" (method=POST OR method=DELETE) | eval segments = split(uri_path, "/") | eval last_seg = mvindex(segments, -1) | eval prev_seg = mvindex(segments, -2) | eval is_root_post = if(method="POST" AND prev_seg="data", 1, 0) | eval is_batch = if(last_seg="batch_save", 1, 0) | eval action = case( method="DELETE", "delete", is_batch=1, "bulk_save", is_root_post=1, "create", method="POST", "update", 1=1, method) | eval collection = case( is_batch=1, mvindex(segments, -3), is_root_post=1, last_seg, 1=1, prev_seg) | eval kv_key = case( is_batch=1 OR is_root_post=1, "-", 1=1, last_seg) | sort - _time | head 200 | table _time, user, action, collection, kv_key, status, uri_path | rename kv_key as "_key", uri_path as "URI"
